what_it_does: A standalone preview card (a @dsCard, group 'Brand') that displays the gold accent colour scale as six swatch chips with names and hex values, plus a lead paragraph explaining gold's role as the system's single saturated 'active/decision' accent.
observations: HTML comment header `@dsCard group="Brand" name="Gold scale" subtitle=... viewport="700x200"` — metadata for a gallery harness.
Links a shared `_card.css` stylesheet and adds card-local inline CSS using design tokens (--font-body, --fg-secondary, --r-md, --border-faint, etc.).
Six tiers shown with literal hardcoded hex backgrounds: gold-50 #FBF6DC, gold-soft #F4ECC4, gold-dashed #D8C040, gold #D8B820 (starred ★), gold-hover #C0A218, gold-press #9C8413.
Lead text states gold is reserved for Vi, primary actions, active borders, selected states, link emphasis — and is never a default background.
References gold as a ramp (gold→bronze→tan, --ramp-1..4) used to position sequence & depth; the six tiers are its interaction states.
standouts: Names the gold accent as 'the one saturated voice, reserved for Vi' — a strong design-doctrine statement embedded in a swatch card.
The starred (★) gold-#D8B820 marks the base/default tier among the six.
conventions: Follows the @dsCard gallery convention (comment metadata + _card.css + viewport).
Uses design tokens for typography/borders but hardcodes the actual swatch hex values inline (expected for a colour-definition card displaying the literal values).
notes: A gallery swatch card defining and explaining the gold/bronze/tan accent ramp and its six interaction tiers.
questions: Where are these gold hex values canonically defined as tokens (the card hardcodes them) and do --ramp-1..4 map to this scale?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the @dsCard metadata says 'Gold scale / Primary brand accent — 6 tiers' and the body shows exactly six gold tiers with usage doctrine.
apparent_themes: Single saturated accent reserved for active/decision states
Tokenised colour scale with interaction tiers (base/hover/press)
intention_signals: Aims to document and visually anchor the brand's one accent colour, teaching both its tiers and its strict usage rule (decision accent only, never a default background).
novelty: The explicit doctrine that gold is 'reserved for Vi' and is a sequence/depth ramp, not just a palette entry.
contribution: Contributes a visual + doctrinal definition of the gold accent ramp (six tiers, hex values, usage rule) that a fused system would keep as the canonical accent-colour reference card.
summary_for_embedding: A Brand-group design-system preview card showing the six-tier gold accent scale with hex values and a usage doctrine that gold is the single saturated decision accent reserved for Vi and active states.
intention_for_embedding: To define and explain the brand's one saturated accent colour, its interaction tiers, and its strict usage rule within the design system.
